extraction.text:
Marketing Campaign  
The Hook:  VMware’s recent acquisition and licensing changes have created uncertainty. Customers 
are facing "VMware tax" hikes.  
The Solution:  OpenShift (for containers/VMs), Rancher (for multi -cluster management), and Harvester 
(for VM virtualizatio n) provide a Kubernetes -native  future that frees them from proprietary hardware 
and licensing jail.  
Part 1: Social Media Posts (LinkedIn & Facebook)  
Option A: The "Wake Up Call"  
Focus: The financial & operational ri sk of staying put.  
Headline:  Is your VMware bill about to explode?  
The honeymoon with traditional virtualization is over. With recent licensing upheavals and the 
aggressive push toward VMware Cloud Foundation, many IT leaders are facing a 300% co st increase 
with no end in sight.  
It’s time to talk about your exit strategy.  
We are helping businesses break free from the "VMware tax" by migrating to a modern, open -
source ecosystem:  
➢ OpenShift:  Run VMs and containers side -by-side.  
➢ Rancher:  Centralized management for Kubernetes clusters.  
➢ Harvester:  A hyper -converged infrastructure built for the cloud -native era.  
Stop paying a premium for legacy architecture. Future -proof your data center with solutions that put 
you back in control.  
#VMware #OpenShift #Rancher #Kubernetes #CloudNative #ITInfrastructure #TechStrategy  
Option B: The Modernization  Angle  
Focus: Agility and innovation. We p air this with a clean graphic comparing "Old School" vs. "Cloud 
Native."  
Headline : Your infrastructure should accelerate innovation, not hold it hostage.  
For decades, VMware was the gold standard for virtualizatio n. But today’s applications demand a 
new approach —one that bridges the gap between traditional VMs and modern containers.  
Enter the Red Hat & Rancher Power Stack:  
➢ OpenShift:  The industry’s most comprehensive enterprise Kubernetes platform.  
➢ Harvester:  Deliver VM services on an open, hyper -converged infrastructure.  
Imagine managing your entire data center  VMs, containers, and bare metal  from a single pane of 
glass. That’s not just cost savings; that's a competitive advantage.  
Ready to moder nize? Let’s talk about your migration roadmap.  
#DevOps #HybridCloud #OpenSource #Rancher #Harvester #VMwareMigration  
Part 2: Email Campaign (To Specific Customers)  
 
Subject Line s Strategy:  
➢ High Priorit y: Your VMware renewal is coming up. Let’s talk options.  
➢ Curiosity:  The opposite of "VMware tax."  
➢ Direct:  Your 3 -year plan to move off VMware (starts here).  
Email Body Template : 
Subject:  Re: Any From The Subject Lines Strategy  
Hi {Name} , 
I’ve been watching the VMware/Broadcom situation closely, and I know your team is likely doing the 
same.  
The changes to the licensing structure aren't just a pricing tweak; they represent a fundamental shift 
in how VMware wants to lock in enterprise customers. For many IT leaders I speak with, the question 
is no longer "if" they will diversify their virtualization stack, but "how"  and "how fast."  
Given your investment in [their industry or known tech stack ], I wanted to share a strategic 
alternative that is gaining serious traction in the market.  
1. The Modern Alternative: The Open Hybrid Cloud Stac k 
Instead of paying escalating costs for a proprietary hypervisor, you can move to a Kubernetes -native 
infrastructure that treats VMs as just another workload. This is where OpenShift, Rancher, and 
Harvester  come in.  
➢ For your virtualized worklo ads: Harvester provides enterprise -class HCI functionality built on 
Kubernetes, allowing you to run your traditional VMs alongside modern containers without 
the VMware license tax.  
➢ For your developers:  OpenShift gives them a self -service platform t o deploy applications 
faster.  
➢ For your operations:  Rancher unifies all your Kubernetes clusters, whether they are on -
prem, in the cloud, or at the edge.  
Why now?  
By starting a migration now, you control the timeline. You can run OpenShift/Harv ester side -by-side 
with VMware  today and begin a phased, risk -free migration over the next 12 -24 months.  
I’ve put together a brief, no -pressure overview comparing your current VMware TCO against a 
modern OpenShift/Harvester architecture. Would you be op en to a 15 -minute call next week to walk 
through it?  
Let me know what time works for you.  
Best,  
Name, Ti tle 
Link to Calendar
